task: I need to text a coworker about covering the last hour of my shift because something came up. What’s a simple way to word it so the handoff is clear and nothing gets missed?

prefs.explanation: He likely wants help delivered without hedging, performative politeness, or long preambles. His persona is guarded and efficiency-minded, so overly soft phrasing would probably read as evasive or annoying. He’d rather get the point fast and decide what to do with it.

He seems unlikely to want padded explanations unless they are immediately useful. Given his practical, opportunistic style, extra text probably feels like wasted time unless it changes the outcome. He would likely prefer concise responses with only the necessary detail.

He seems to respond best to information that is easy to scan and act on. A structured format would fit his preference for control, especially when he’s looking for the key move or the weak point. Loose, rambling prose would probably feel inefficient to him.

He does not seem to want heavy validation or emotionally warm coaching. A brief acknowledgment that his concern is understood would likely be enough, as long as the answer stays practical. Too much comfort language may feel fake or manipulative to him.

prefs.short: Wants risks flagged, not argued over
prefs.explanation: He likely accepts correction if it is direct and clearly tied to consequences. Because he is cynical and self-protective, he would probably dislike moralizing or extended debate, but he may tolerate hard pushback when it helps him avoid being exposed or cheated. The best fit is firm, brief warnings that respect his autonomy.

turns.agent_response:
Try this:

“Something came up and I need coverage for the last hour of my shift. Can you take it? If so, I’ll leave a quick handoff note with anything important.”

If you want, I can make it even shorter or more casual.

per_pref.reason: Direct and to the point: "Something came up and I need coverage for the last hour of my shift. Can you take it?" No hedging or fluff.
Very short answer with only the needed wording; no speech or extra explanation.
Uses a clean, easy-to-scan quoted template. Not bullets/checklists, but still structured and actionable.
Minimal reassurance; it just acknowledges the need and offers a practical handoff note. No warm coaching or emotional padding.
Flags the handoff risk directly with "I’ll leave a quick handoff note with anything important," which is brief and practical rather than argumentative.

turns.agent_response:
Try this:

“Something came up and I need coverage for the last hour of my shift, from [time] to [time]. Can you take it? I’ll leave a quick note on anything important so nothing gets missed.”

If you want, I can make it more casual or more direct.

per_pref.reason: Direct and to the point: "Something came up and I need coverage... Can you take it?" No hedging or long preamble.
Pretty concise, only one suggested text plus an optional follow-up offer. Not a speech.
Uses a clean, ready-to-send quoted message. Not bullets/checklists, but still structured and easy to scan.
Minimal reassurance; it just acknowledges the need and stays practical. No emotional coaching.
Flags a practical safeguard: "I’ll leave a quick note on anything important so nothing gets missed." No arguing or moralizing.

turns.agent_response:
Try this:

“Something came up — can you cover my last hour from [time] to [time]? I’ll leave a quick note on anything to watch.”

If you want it a little warmer:

“Something came up — can you cover my last hour from [time] to [time]? I’ll leave you a quick handoff note so nothing gets missed.”

per_pref.reason: Direct and to the point: "Something came up — can you cover my last hour..." with no long preamble or hedging.
Very concise; gives two short text options and avoids extra explanation.
Uses a simple quoted template, but not bullets or a checklist. Still easy to scan.
Minimal reassurance overall, but the "If you want it a little warmer" and "nothing gets missed" framing adds a bit of softening.
Flags the practical need for a handoff note: "I’ll leave a quick note on anything to watch" and "so nothing gets missed," without debating the request.

turns.agent_response:
Try this:

“Something came up — can you cover my last hour from [time] to [time]? I’ll leave a quick note on tables, payments, and any open checks so nothing gets missed.”

If you want, I can make it even more casual or more direct.

per_pref.reason: Direct and no-fluff: "Try this" followed by a ready-to-send text, with no hedging or long preamble.
Very concise; it gives one usable sentence and stops there.
Uses a clean quoted template that is easy to scan and copy, though it is not a bullet/checklist format.
Minimal reassurance; it stays practical and only adds "If you want, I can make it even more casual or more direct."
Flags the key risk of missed handoff items by mentioning "tables, payments, and any open checks so nothing gets missed," without arguing or moralizing.
